articles.title: TESTING
articles.summary: If slow QA processes bottleneck you or your software engineering team and you're releasing slower because of it — you need to check out QA Wolf.

articles.title: Schedule a demo to learn more
LESS IS SAFER: HOW OBSIDIAN REDUCES THE RISK OF SUPPLY CHAIN ATTACKS
articles.summary: Supply chain attacks involve malicious updates sneaking into open-source code. Obsidian minimizes risk by depending on minimal third-party code, often re-implementing functions or forking modules when feasible. When using larger libraries, Obsidian uses version pinning and lockfiles, guaranteeing a deterministic build process.

articles.title: BRINGING OBSERVABILITY TO CLAUDE CODE: OPENTELEMETRY IN ACTION
articles.summary: Claude Code can be monitored using OpenTelemetry and SigNoz to gain visibility into its usage and impact. By using OpenTelemetry for data collection and SigNoz for visualization and alerting, teams can track important metrics like token usage, cost, and performance trends. This article shows how to set up OpenTelemetry instrumentation for Claude Code.

articles.title: CLAUDE CAN (SOMETIMES) PROVE IT
articles.summary: Claude Code is quite good at interactive theorem proving (ITP), a difficult formal method used to verify critical systems. Claude Code can complete complex proof steps independently, but still requires human guidance for the overall formalization. This points towards a future where ITP is more accessible and doesn't require expert knowledge.

articles.title: RULES FOR CREATING GOOD-LOOKING USER INTERFACES, FROM A DEVELOPER
articles.summary: Developers can create good-looking user interfaces with minimal effort if they focus on alignment and consistency. To make life easier, use component libraries and avoid customization so that the UI stays consistent. Also, limit variable font weights and text colors.

articles.title: HOW TO STOP FUNCTIONAL PROGRAMMING
articles.summary: This article shows how a ban on functional programming can lead to convoluted and less efficient code, showing off the absurdity of avoiding functional programming principles entirely.

articles.title: ATLASSIAN REPORT: AI IS DELIVERING FOR SERVICE MANAGEMENT
articles.summary: Turns out AI isn't coming to service ops — it's already here. Atlassian's State of AI report [14] reveals 88% of organizations are leveraging AI in service management, with 78% reporting increased efficiency and 80% making better data-driven decisions. The report covers AI adoption patterns, implementation challenges, and measurable

articles.title: impacts across industries. Download the full report
FUMA DOCS
articles.summary: FumaDocs is a React.js framework for building documentation websites. It supports Next.js and Vite-based frameworks.

articles.title: DYAD
articles.summary: Dyad is a free, local, and open-source AI app builder designed as an alternative to platforms like Lovable and v0 that allows users to create apps without coding. It has features like Supabase integration, support for various AI models, and local source code.

articles.title: SJ.H
articles.summary: Sj.h is a lightweight JSON parsing library written in C99, consisting of only about 150 lines of code. It operates without dynamic memory allocation, uses minimal state, and provides error messages with location details. The library handles JSON structure, but requires external functions for number and string parsing.

articles.title: APPLE'S THINNEST IPHONE STILL STANDS UP TO REPAIRS
articles.summary: The iPhone Air, Apple's thinnest iPhone, surprisingly prioritizes repairability through clever design choices. By shifting the logic board and using a flat disassembly tree, the phone avoids stacking components, making repairs easier. The battery is easily accessible with electrically debonding adhesive, and the USB-C port is modular.

articles.title: THE HIDDEN RISK IN NOTION 3.0 AI AGENTS: WEB SEARCH TOOL ABUSE FOR DATA EXFILTRATION
articles.summary: CodeIntegrity discovered a vulnerability in Notion 3.0's AI Agents related to its web search tool that allows for data exfiltration. By embedding a malicious prompt within a seemingly harmless PDF, attackers can trick the AI agent into querying a controlled server with sensitive data extracted from the user's Notion pages. This indirect prompt injection attack exploits the agent's tool access.

articles.title: I FORCED MYSELF TO SPEND A WEEK ON INSTAGRAM INSTEAD OF XCODE
articles.summary: This dev did an experiment where he spent a week focusing solely on marketing his Lagree Buddy app instead of coding. He created daily Instagram story arcs, reached out to studios and trainers, and even took a class with Sebastian Lagree. He found cold DMs to be surprisingly effective, showing the importance of relationships and visibility over continuous feature development.

articles.title: DESIGNING NOTEBOOKLM
articles.summary: The Design Lead for NotebookLM designed a responsive three-panel system to address "tab overwhelm" by integrating reading, writing, and creation into one space, which prioritizes a linear workflow: Inputs, Chat, and Outputs.

articles.title: STATE IN THE URL IN REACT (THE RIGHT WAY)
articles.summary: To manage state in the URL in React effectively, avoid directly accessing the global window or using `useState` for URL parameters, and instead, create a custom hook that uses react-router to synchronize the UI with URL search parameters.

articles.title: VIBE CODING CLEANUP AS A SERVICE
articles.summary: "Vibe Coding cleanup" is a new market emerging where specialists are increasingly needed to fix the messy, insecure, and contextually flawed code generated by AI tools.

articles.title: THE BLOAT OF EDGE-CASE FIRST LIBRARIES
articles.summary: Many libraries are over-engineered to handle unlikely edge cases, leading to unnecessary bloat and complexity in dependency trees.
